Pathway_Genes.Variety: Williams 82 (W82); hairy root assays in Kefeng-1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: mGWAS using resequencing SNP data from previous study (53 soja around 1× coverage included); metabolomics by LC-qToF-MS for seed isoflavonoid profiling of 727 accessions in 2013 and multi-location samples in 2014; transcriptome profiling in infected hypocotyls; LC-QQQ-MS/MS stable-isotope tracing with daidzein-d6; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Preferentially expressed in mature roots and hypocotyls of soybean seedlings; expression pattern mirrors glycitein-type isoflavonoid accumulation; not induced or repressed by Phytophthora sojae infection
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression lines; native promoter in endogenous gene/haplotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring C6 hydroxylation of daidzein to form 6-hydroxydaidzein, which is subsequently 6-O-methylated to glycitein; also shows in vitro activity toward formononetin and biochanin A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature roots and hypocotyls of soybean seedlings; also present in seeds as glycosides/malonylglycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against Phytophthora sojae as phytoanticipin-related metabolites; soybean isoflavones also noted as beneficial to human health/nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; daidzein-d6 feeding; germinating seed hypocotyl stage; heterologous yeast substrate feeding; ion effects on enzyme activity (Mn2+ and Ca2+ enhance, Fe2+/Fe3+/Cu2+ inhibit)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GmIF6H1 nearly abolishes glycitein-type isoflavonoids and increases daidzin/malonyl-daidzin; overexpression elevates 6-hydroxydaidzein conjugates and raises glycitein during infection; both loss and overexpression increase susceptibility to P. sojae, showing that proper tuning of flux between glycitein and glyceollin is required
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of glycitein biosynthesis; branch-allocation control point for daidzein partitioning between glycitein-type isoflavonoids and glyceollins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful target for engineering soybean isoflavonoid composition, disease resistance, and health-related seed quality; haplotype selection and precise tuning rather than simple overexpression is important
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces glyceollin I accumulation during P. sojae infection and increases susceptibility; excessive or insufficient activity causes defense imbalance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally referred to as GmCYP76F17 and redesigned as GmIF6H1. A T248A substitution distinguishes major haplotypes; Thr248 (Hap2) confers higher catalytic efficiency than Ala248 (Hap1) and is associated with higher glycitein-type isoflavonoids. Km toward daidzein was 7.49 ± 0.73 µM. Although in vitro activity toward formononetin and biochanin A is higher, these substrates/products were not detected in soybean hypocotyls under tested conditions, so daidzein is the bona fide in planta substrate. Trace glycitein in infected mutants suggests possible minor alternative routes under specific conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIF6H1 (Glyma.11g108300/CYP76F17) was identified by mGWAS as the major locus associated with glycitein-type isoflavonoids in soybean seeds. 2. Biochemical assays demonstrated that GmIF6H1 is an ER-localized isoflavone 6-hydroxylase that converts daidzein to 6-hydroxydaidzein, establishing a predominant biosynthetic route to glycitein. 3. CRISPR loss-of-function mutants nearly lacked glycitein-type isoflavonoids, whereas overexpression caused accumulation of 6-hydroxydaidzein conjugates and increased glycitein during infection. 4. Stable-isotope tracing with daidzein-d6 proved daidzein is the bona fide in planta substrate for glycitein biosynthesis via 6-hydroxydaidzein. 5. Natural variation at residue 248 (Thr versus Ala) changes enzyme efficiency and underlies domestication-associated reduction of glycitein-type isoflavonoids. 6. Both deficient and excessive GmIF6H1 activity increase susceptibility to P. sojae because GmIF6H1 controls daidzein allocation between glycitein-type phytoanticipins and glyceollin phytoalexins.

Title: A balance between glycitein and glyceollins governed by isoflavone 6-hydroxylase confers soybean resistance to Phytophthora sojae
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
